title: Capura multijuga
taxonomy: Capura multijuga is a species of plant that belongs to the family Melastomataceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in countries such as India and Sri Lanka. It is an evergreen shrub that grows in the wild, typically found in forests and along streams. The classification of Capura multijuga is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and then into the class Magnoliopsida. Further research is required to confirm the exact classification.
uses: Capura multijuga is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its medicinal properties are said to help with various health issues, including fever and skin conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory problems and as an antiseptic.
